findings.finding: Current best practice uses rolling 180-day windows to compute per-signal IC (Spearman rank correlation between signal value and N-day forward return), then weights signals proportional to their trailing IC. A 'Mega-Alpha' approach filters and re-weights recently effective alphas via linear regression at each rebalance, discarding stale signals automatically. Key metrics: IC, ICIR (IC Information Ratio = mean(IC)/std(IC)), RIC (Rank IC). Bounded regression caps any single signal contribution to enforce diversification. XGBoost trained on top-IC alphas as a nonlinear combiner outperforms linear IC-weighting in walk-forward tests (arxiv 2512.12924). PPO-based adaptive alpha weighting (arxiv 2509.01393) uses deep RL to continuously mine and re-weight signals in non-stationary markets.
findings.relevance_to_us: We currently use equal weights across 21 active signals. Switching to trailing-IC weighting would upweight our 60-68% accuracy regime signals (ADX, Amihud, Choppiness) and downweight noisy ones (RSI at 52%, Mean Reversion at 52.6%). The 180-day rolling window aligns with our existing accuracy_cache.json 30-day recalc. Implementation: compute per-signal IC in outcome_tracker.py, store in accuracy_cache, use IC as weight in signal_engine.py consensus.
findings.expected_impact: high — estimated 3-5 ppt improvement in consensus accuracy based on literature
findings.source: arxiv 2512.12924, arxiv 2509.01393, quantbeckman.com walk-forward guide, alphascientist.com

findings.finding: Research confirms that buy and sell signals should be treated as separate prediction problems. Asymmetric loss functions (penalizing wrong-direction forecasts more heavily) improve directional accuracy by 3.4-6.1 ppt over MSE/MAE (MDPI 2673-2688/6/10/268). Practical approaches: (1) Maintain separate accuracy stats for BUY vs SELL per signal — a signal with 70% SELL accuracy but 30% BUY accuracy should only emit SELL, with BUY suppressed. (2) Direction-specific confidence multipliers: if a signal's BUY accuracy is below random (50%), its BUY votes get 0 weight (not inverted — inversion causes whiplash, which our system already learned). (3) Coherence weighting: amplify signal when directional components agree, attenuate when they conflict. (4) Asymmetric exit rules: more conservative exits for bearish signals even without confirmation, since downside moves are faster.
findings.relevance_to_us: CRITICAL. Our BUY signals are 26-45% accurate vs SELL at 60-75%. Current system treats both directions equally. Fix: split accuracy_cache.json into per-direction stats. In signal_engine.py, apply direction-specific weight: if signal X has BUY accuracy < 47%, its BUY votes get weight 0 (force HOLD on buy side only). SELL votes from same signal keep full weight. This alone should dramatically reduce false BUY signals dragging down consensus.
findings.expected_impact: very high — directly addresses our worst failure mode (false BUY signals)

findings.finding: The regime detection AI market hit $1.84B in 2025 (CAGR 23.8%). Modern implementations use ensemble HMMs (Gaussian + Variational Bayesian) with adaptive model selection at each timestep plus smoothing to prevent noise-driven regime flips. Key finding: regime-conditioned strategies achieve higher Sharpe and lower drawdown across all phases. Statistical Jump Model approach (arxiv 2402.05272) uses jump detection for regime shifts — we already have this as signal #14 (Statistical Jump Regime, 54.3% accuracy). Dynamic Factor Allocation (arxiv 2410.14841) leverages regime-switching signals to rotate factor exposure. Two-Level Uncertainty framework (arxiv 2603.13252) adds safety via uncertainty quantification when alpha breaks in regime transitions.
findings.relevance_to_us: We have 6 regime signals (ADX, Amihud, Choppiness, BOCPD, Vol Ratio, Drift Regime Gate) averaging 60-68% accuracy — our best performers. Current use: regime penalties (0.75x ranging, 0.80x high-vol) applied uniformly. Improvement: instead of just penalizing, use regime state to SELECT which non-regime signals are active. E.g., in trending regime (ADX>25), enable momentum/trend signals and disable mean-reversion; in ranging regime, do the opposite. This is signal SELECTION not just weighting.
findings.expected_impact: high — regime-conditional selection is the #1 differentiator in institutional quant systems

findings.finding: Gold-silver ratio (GSR) is the primary mean-reversion vehicle for precious metals. Current GSR ~60:1 (down from 105:1 in Apr 2025). Historical mean-reversion range: 40:1 to 90:1, long-term average 60-70:1. At current ~60, ratio is near fair value — less mean-reversion alpha available than when it was at 88-105. Best indicators: RSI on GSR (not on silver alone), Bollinger Bands on GSR, rate-of-change of GSR. Silver seasonality: January (65% positive) and October (68% positive) are strongest months; mid-year weakness June-August. Since 2020, pattern shifted: Feb weakest, March strong. Supply deficit (6th consecutive year, 100-120M oz annual shortfall) creates structural bid. Solar demand >200M oz/year by end-2026. Industrial demand (55% of total) dominates seasonal pattern.
findings.relevance_to_us: Our Mean Reversion signal (52.6% accuracy) applies generic RSI(2/3)/IBS/BB%B to silver. Should add GSR-specific mean reversion: compute GSR, apply RSI/BB on the ratio instead of raw price. Also add seasonality overlay (our calendar signal is disabled). Supply deficit context should feed into prophecy.json conviction. Current silver prophecy ($120, 0.8 conviction) aligns with analyst consensus ($87-100 base case).
findings.expected_impact: medium — GSR mean reversion is well-established, but current ratio near fair value limits near-term alpha
findings.source: fxstreet.com GSR analysis, usagold.com GSR guide, investinghaven.com seasonality, fxempire.com silver forecast

findings.finding: MVRV Z-Score reliability questioned: peaked at only 2.524 in Jan 2025 (vs 4.0 in 2017, 3.2 in 2021), suggesting cycle dynamics changing with ETF adoption. SOPR + exchange flow composite achieved 74% directional accuracy over 7-day horizons in 2025 (Glassnode). New developments: (1) ETF flow integration — $125B AUM spot BTC ETFs create new holder category requiring separate tracking. (2) Composite scoring: 5 of 7 core metrics flashing bottom signals as of Mar 2026 (MVRV at 1.2, aSOPR at 0.97-0.99, exchange reserves at 7-year low). (3) Best ML approach: Temporal Fusion Transformers combining MVRV, SOPR, exchange flows, options IV, and macro indicators — outperforms standalone on-chain signals. (4) Bitcoin Barometer: 27 on-chain metrics condensed to one weekly score. (5) Historical pattern: MVRV 1.0-1.5 band reversals produced positive 12-month forward returns 7/7 times since 2013.
findings.relevance_to_us: Our On-Chain BTC signal (#13, 60% accuracy, BTC-only) uses MVRV, SOPR, NUPL, Exchange Netflow. Two improvements: (1) Add ETF flow data as on-chain proxy — significant new signal source. (2) Use SOPR+exchange flow composite (74% accuracy per Glassnode) instead of individual metrics. (3) Consider Temporal Fusion Transformer approach for combining on-chain with our other signals. Current MVRV at 1.2 suggests we're in a historically positive accumulation zone.
findings.expected_impact: medium — on-chain already at 60% accuracy, improvements likely incremental
findings.source: neutralis.finance on-chain metrics, spotedcrypto.com bottom signals, nansen.ai onchain metrics, altcointrading.net ML models

recommended_improvements.title: Direction-Specific Accuracy Gating
recommended_improvements.description: Split accuracy tracking into BUY accuracy and SELL accuracy per signal per ticker. In signal_engine.py, apply direction-specific weight: if BUY accuracy < 47% (30+ samples), that signal's BUY vote gets weight 0 (becomes HOLD). SELL votes unaffected. This directly addresses the 26-45% BUY vs 60-75% SELL asymmetry without inversion.
recommended_improvements.priority: P0 — highest impact, addresses worst failure mode

recommended_improvements.title: Trailing-IC Signal Weighting
recommended_improvements.description: Replace equal-weight consensus with IC-weighted consensus. Compute rolling 90-day IC (rank correlation of signal value vs 1d/3d forward return) per signal per ticker. Store in accuracy_cache.json alongside existing accuracy. In signal_engine.py, weight each signal's vote by max(0, trailing_IC) instead of 1.0. Signals with zero or negative IC contribute nothing.
recommended_improvements.priority: P1 — high impact, well-supported by literature

recommended_improvements.title: Regime-Conditional Signal Activation
recommended_improvements.description: Use regime signal outputs (ADX, Choppiness, Drift Gate) to conditionally enable/disable non-regime signals. Define signal subsets: trending_signals = [Momentum, EMA, MACD], ranging_signals = [Mean Reversion, BB, RSI]. When regime = trending (ADX>25 + Choppiness<38.2), only trending_signals vote; when ranging, only ranging_signals vote. Regime-agnostic signals (News, On-Chain, F&G) always vote.
recommended_improvements.priority: P2 — high impact but complex, requires careful backtesting

recommended_improvements.title: Gold-Silver Ratio Mean Reversion Signal
recommended_improvements.description: Add GSR-specific mean reversion signal for XAG/XAU. Compute GSR from live prices, apply RSI(14) and BB(20,2) on ratio (not raw price). BUY silver when GSR > 80 + RSI(GSR) > 70, SELL silver when GSR < 50 + RSI(GSR) < 30. More reliable than raw price mean reversion for metals.

recommended_improvements.title: SOPR+Exchange Flow Composite for BTC
recommended_improvements.description: Replace individual MVRV/SOPR/Netflow inputs in on-chain signal with Glassnode-style composite scoring. Weight SOPR(0.3) + Exchange Netflow(0.3) + MVRV(0.2) + NUPL(0.2). The composite achieved 74% 7d directional accuracy in 2025 testing. Add ETF flow data as supplementary input when available.
recommended_improvements.priority: P2 — incremental improvement on already-decent signal
